title: smallanthus connatus
intro: Smallanthus connatus, also known as wild sunchokes or Jerusalem artichokes, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to North America. The plant's edible tubers have been used for centuries in traditional medicine, boasting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming wild sunchokes include lowering cholesterol levels, regulating blood sugar, and providing antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, wild sunchokes can be eaten raw or cooked, or used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm their efficacy and optimal preparation methods.
